use tokio::sync::Mutex;
use std::sync::Arc;

struct Service {
    users: Arc<Mutex<UserStore>>,
    sessions: Arc<Mutex<SessionStore>>,
}

impl Service {
    async fn login(&self, user_id: u64) {
        let user = {
            let users = self.users.lock().await;
            users.find(user_id)
        };
        let sessions = self.sessions.lock().await;
        sessions.create(user);
    }

    async fn logout(&self, session_id: &str) {
        let user_id = {
            let sessions = self.sessions.lock().await;
            sessions.get(session_id).user_id
        };
        let users = self.users.lock().await;
        users.mark_inactive(user_id);
    }
}

struct UserStore;
impl UserStore {
    fn find(&self, _id: u64) -> User { todo!() }
    fn mark_inactive(&self, _id: u64) { todo!() }
}

struct SessionStore;
impl SessionStore {
    fn create(&self, _user: User) { todo!() }
    fn get(&self, _id: &str) -> Session { todo!() }
}

struct User;
struct Session { user_id: u64 }
